philosophy.label: Philosophy
philosophy.quote:
"Every inhale is a universe
expanding. Every exhale,
a cosmos remembering itself."
philosophy.body1: The human body breathes approximately 20,000 times each day — a rhythm so ancient it predates consciousness itself. When we place the anatomical atlas beside the sacred geometric canon, something astonishing emerges: the branching of bronchi mirrors the fractal precision of the Sierpinski triangle.
philosophy.body2: Anatomy of Breath is a meditative inquiry into this convergence — a visual philosophy that honors both the scientific and the numinous, recognizing that the two have always been speaking the same language.

gallery.label: Collection
gallery.title: The Living
gallery.subtitle: Tap to reveal sacred geometry within

lungField.title: The Lung Field
lungField.subtitle: Pulmonary Expansion
lungField.description: The lungs are not merely organs of respiration — they are the body's most direct interface with the invisible world. Each breath draws the cosmos inward, and the Vesica Piscis mirrors this duality: two realms overlapping in the sacred space between.

bronchialTree.title: Bronchial Tree
bronchialTree.subtitle: Sacred Branching
bronchialTree.description: The bronchial tree branches with the same mathematical precision as lightning, river deltas, and the Star of David. Nature does not repeat itself — it rhymes. Every bifurcation is a choice, a sacred division of the whole into its parts.

diaphragm.title: Diaphragm
diaphragm.subtitle: The First Mover
diaphragm.description: The diaphragm is the body's first mover — the prime muscle of life. Its dome-shaped arc echoes Metatron's Cube, the geometric blueprint said to contain all forms of creation. To breathe consciously is to activate this sacred architecture.

alveolarBloom.title: Alveolar Bloom
alveolarBloom.subtitle: The Flower Within
alveolarBloom.description: Three hundred million alveoli bloom inside each lung — a surface area the size of a tennis court, folded into the space of your fist. The Sri Yantra's interlocking triangles mirror this impossible compression of infinity into form.

neuralBreath.title: Neural Breath
neuralBreath.subtitle: The Nervous Rhythm
neuralBreath.description: The phrenic nerve descends from the cervical spine, threading through the thorax to animate the diaphragm. This neural pathway is the body's telegraph line between mind and breath — consciousness made physical through the pentagram's five-pointed symmetry.

theSilence.title: The Silence
theSilence.subtitle: Between Inhale & Exhale
theSilence.description: Between the exhale and the next inhale exists a pause — the kumbhaka. In this stillness, the torus field of the body completes its cycle. The silence is not empty; it is the fullest moment, the still point around which all breath revolves.

sacredHeart.title: The Sacred Heart
sacredHeart.subtitle: Pulse of Existence
sacredHeart.description: The heart beats approximately 100,000 times each day, never resting, never pausing. The Flower of Life — seven interlocking circles — is said to encode the pattern of creation itself. The heart does not pump blood; it sings it into motion.

spinalColumn.title: Spinal Column
spinalColumn.subtitle: The Axis of Being
spinalColumn.description: The spinal column is the body's axis mundi — the world tree, the ladder between earth and sky. The Kabbalistic Tree of Life maps ten sephiroth along a similar vertical axis, each node a center of consciousness, each vertebra a gateway.

ribCage.title: Rib Cage
ribCage.subtitle: The Temple of Breath
ribCage.description: The rib cage is a living temple — twelve pairs of arches protecting the sacred organs within. The golden spiral encoded in its structure follows the same ratio found in nautilus shells, galaxies, and the unfolding of a fern.

cranialVault.title: Cranial Vault
cranialVault.subtitle: The Seat of Consciousness
cranialVault.description: The cranial vault houses the most complex structure in the known universe. Metatron's Cube, with its thirteen circles and seventy-eight lines, is said to contain the geometric blueprint of all matter — a fitting overlay for the seat of consciousness.

vascularRivers.title: Vascular Rivers
vascularRivers.subtitle: The Flow of Life
vascularRivers.description: Sixty thousand miles of blood vessels thread through the human body — enough to circle the Earth twice. The Sri Yantra's nine interlocking triangles create forty-three smaller triangles, mirroring the fractal branching of every capillary bed.

innerEye.title: The Inner Eye
innerEye.subtitle: Window of the Soul
innerEye.description: The eye contains 130 million photoreceptors, each one a transducer converting light into meaning. The golden spiral — nature's most elegant ratio — governs the iris's expansion and contraction, the same mathematics that governs galaxies in their turning.

steps.title: The First Movement
steps.subtitle: Diaphragm Descends
steps.description: The diaphragm — a dome of muscle — contracts and flattens. Pressure inside the thorax drops below atmospheric. Air rushes in through the nose, warming and filtering as it travels the 11cm of the nasal passage.

steps.title: The Tracheal River
steps.subtitle: Air Enters the Airways
steps.description: Warmed to 37°C and humidified to 100%, air descends the trachea — a 12cm tube reinforced by C-shaped cartilage rings.

steps.title: The Lung Field Opens
steps.subtitle: Alveolar Inflation
steps.description: Air fans into 300 million alveoli — each a sphere 0.2mm across. The total surface area unfolds to 70m².

steps.title: The Sacred Transfer
steps.subtitle: Oxygen Crosses the Membrane
steps.description: Across a membrane just 0.5 microns thick, oxygen dissolves into the blood while carbon dioxide flows out.

steps.title: The Vascular Journey
steps.subtitle: Oxygen Enters the Blood
steps.description: Haemoglobin binds oxygen and transports it through the bloodstream to the heart and body.

steps.title: The Cellular Flame
steps.subtitle: Mitochondrial Respiration
steps.description: Inside each cell, oxygen is used to produce ATP — the energy that powers life.

vault.description: The Vault will open its doors soon. Leave your email to be the first to know.

hero.title: Soma
hero.subtitle: Body therapies, breathwork and movement
hero.body: We explore the body as sacred territory — a living map of memory, emotion, and transformative potential.

breathwork.title: Breathwork
breathwork.description: Ancestral and contemporary techniques to release, energize, and transform through breath.

movement.title: Conscious Movement
movement.description: Somatic practices that integrate body, mind, and spirit in dynamic flow.

alignment.title: Structural Alignment
alignment.description: Manual and postural therapies to restore the body's natural balance.

site.name: Anatomy of Breath
site.description: Where science dissolves into sacred. A visual philosophy blending anatomy and sacred geometry.
